commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces iOS onboarding’s first gateway screen with a connection-path chooser, adds a Tailscale/manual remote path, persists a plaintext tailnet route flag, and updates gateway auth issue handling/tests.

PR surface: Other +813. Total +813 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding from source inspection: the Tailscale flow probes `link.host`/`link.port`, leaves advanced fields editable, then `connectManual` rereads mutable state. I did not run an iOS simulator repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer/contributor action is needed because the PR has a protected label, proof links are unavailable, and the remaining blocker touches a security-sensitive plaintext route exception.

target.title: feat(ios): improve onboarding gateway connection flow
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

iOS onboarding made gateway connection feel brittle: users had to choose between QR/setup code, LAN discovery, and remote/Tailscale concepts without a clear first-run path. Tailscale also needed to feel like a real path for users who already have a gateway on their tailnet, while keeping the existing QR/deep-link auth contract tight.

## Why This Change Was Made

This replaces the first onboarding screen with a connection-path chooser for QR/setup code, local auto-find, Tailscale/remote, and manual setup. It adds a first-class Tailscale entry path for MagicDNS/Tailscale Serve URLs and guided raw 100.64/10 IP entry, while still keeping generic QR/setup-code/deep-link parsing from accepting plaintext CGNAT setup codes.

It also improves auth recovery copy by distinguishing token-missing, password-missing, and generic unauthorized gateway states, and persists the Tailscale plaintext route context so follow-up retry/reconnect does not silently switch the same raw IP to TLS.

## User Impact

New users get a clearer onboarding decision tree and a smoother path for already-on-tailnet devices: paste a MagicDNS name, Tailscale Serve URL, or tailnet gateway IP from the Tailscale app, then connect. If Tailscale is off, the app now tells the user to turn it on before retrying instead of failing with a vague gateway error.

Simulator proof found an important iOS platform caveat: direct raw `100.x` IP entry reached TCP probe success, but iOS App Transport Security blocked the follow-up plaintext `ws://100.x:18789` connection. MagicDNS/Tailscale Serve reached TLS trust verification and is the green remote path for this PR. The QR/setup-code auth contract remains unchanged: raw gateway token/password are not added to QR output by this PR.

## Evidence

![iOS onboarding P2 visual proof](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba/raw/8f2bc4a587bd23f466de77a04ff8eac81dc71191/ios-onboarding-p2-proof-redacted.gif)

GitHub-hosted redacted proof packet: https://gist.github.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba

Visual proof:
- [First-run welcome](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba/raw/0f4a648e9ef99d7fed9b7933f442f73115812f07/01-first-run-welcome.jpg)
- [Connection path chooser](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba/raw/400989f2cb1d97b4a5967b5e0497f995969ce22f/02-connect-gateway-paths.jpg)
- [Tailscale entry screen](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba/raw/592bf4d814aa96454f65d0641fcb07b151c13639/03-tailscale-entry-screen.jpg)
- [Raw IP ATS caveat screen, redacted](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba/raw/3b746362754ffab67547606b12007b1673b32372/04-tailscale-ip-guard.jpg)
- [MagicDNS/Tailscale Serve trust prompt, redacted](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba/raw/a470721c61094645f999b1eecb4d809b63711fdd/05-tailscale-magicdns-trust-prompt.jpg)
- [Sanitized proof summary](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/f57a46d55fa2037c3a63df2a000249ba/raw/f5d010b0f0b93ceec39c0a738917c1ee632458e8/github-proof-summary-redacted.md)

Code/test proof:
- `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter DeepLinksSecurityTests`
- `xcodebuild -quiet test -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawLogicTests -configuration Debug -destination 'id=056A94B1-69E3-49EA-BA1D-1A0F58D36436' -derivedDataPath apps/ios/build/CodexOnboardingP2LogicTests`
- `xcodebuild -quiet build -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -configuration Debug -destination 'id=056A94B1-69E3-49EA-BA1D-1A0F58D36436' -derivedDataPath apps/ios/build/CodexOnboardingP2Sim`
- XcodeBuildMCP `build_run_sim` on iPhone 17 simulator -> succeeded
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Known existing build warnings remain in unrelated files: `NodeAppModel.swift`, `TalkModeManager.swift`, and `OpenClawChatUI/ChatViewModel.swift`.
